data.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson shot and killed outside Manhattan hotel: reports
data.description:
• UnitedHealthcare (NYSE:UNH) CEO Brian Thompson was shot and killed earlier Monday morning outside a midtown Manhattan hotel where he was scheduled to speak at the company's annual investor conference.
  • Thompson was reportedly shot in the chest by a masked man just outside the Hilton Hotel around 646a local time, the New York Post reported. The alleged assailant then fled down 6th Ave. and has yet to be captured.
  • Thompson was at the hotel as the health insurer's annual investor conference was slated to start at 8a.
  • UnitedHeathcare media relations has not yet responded to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha asking for confirmation and comment on the reported killing.

data.title: Salesforce surges as Agentforce proves to be droids enterprises were looking for
data.description:
Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) CEO Marc Benioff said in September, before the launch of its artificial intelligence-powered Agentforce, that his company had "the droids you are looking for."

His statement is proving to ring true, as Salesforce shares surged more than 10% during early trading Wednesday. The gains come on the back of third quarter fiscal 2025 financial results demonstrating Agentforce is emerging into a strong catalyst for the company.

Shares are currently up 30% since the general release of Agentforce in late October.

During the earnings call last night, Benioff said Salesforce had solidified more than 200 deals during the single week Agentforce was generally available during the third quarter.

"It doesn't mean anything because the pipeline is in the thousands for potential transactions that are coming up in future quarters," Benioff said. "So, companies like FedEx (FDX), Adecco (OTCPK:AHEXF), Accenture (ACN), ACE Hardware, IBM (IBM), RBC Wealth Management and many more are now building their digital labor forces on the Salesforce platform with Agentforce."

"Although the deal commentary will have very minimal impact on 4Q financials, we expect the sales pipeline commentary will only add fuel to the bull thesis into FY26 which, we believe, could have a 1pt uplift to revenue growth with a more meaningful 2pts in FY27 not including the halo affect driving incremental Data Cloud and MuleSoft sales to deliver the data fuel to Agentforce," said Needham analysts, led by Scott Berg, in an investor note.

Needham reiterated its Buy rating and increased its price target to $375 from $345.

Wedbush was even more bullish on the stock, maintaining its Outperform rating while increasing its price target to $425 from $375.

"In essence, Salesforce saw strong underlying growth metrics but importantly, massive demand out of the gates for Agentforce as the AI Party now comes to CRM and the Software World," Wedbush analysts, led Daniel Ives, said in a note.

"Salesforce also introduced its Agentforce Testing Center this quarter representing a first of a kind AI Agent Lifecycle Management Tool for testing autonomous AI agents at scale, providing customers with an opportunity to test, deploy, and monitor agents while scaling further enhancing its growing Agentforce AI product portfolio," he added.

JMP has an even higher price target of $450 on the stock along with its Market Outperform rating.

"Thematically, we agree with computer scientist and Stanford professor Andrew Ng who recently commented that 'even though a lot of attention is on AI technology (especially the foundation models), most of the opportunity will be in building AI applications' and 'the one trend I am most excited about is agentic AI workflows,'" said JPM analyst Patrick Walravens, in a note.

Salesforce competitors were edging higher early on Wednesday as well, including Workday (WDAY) +1.3%, Oracle (ORCL) +2.6% and SAP (SAP) +2%.

data.title: Armstrong World Industries announces acquisition of A. Zahner Company
data.description:
• Armstrong World Industries (NYSE:AWI) has acquired A. Zahner Company.
  • Armstrong funded the acquisition with existing cash and its revolving credit facility. 
  • Zahner offers design, engineering and fabrication of exterior architectural metal solutions. 
  • Zahner has production facilities in Missouri and Texas with ~160 employees. The company is projected to generate revenue of ~$42M in 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stoke Therapeutics gets FDA breakthrough therapy designation for the treatment of dravet syndrome
data.description:
• Stoke Therapeutics (NASDAQ:STOK) received Breakthrough Therapy Designation from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for zorevunersen for the treatment of Dravet syndrome with a confirmed mutation, not associated with gain-of-function, in the SCN1A gene.
  • Zorevunersen is being developed as potentially the first disease-modifying medicine for the treatment of Dravet syndrome.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Eli Lilly’s Zepbound outperforms Novo's Wegovy for weight loss in head-to-head trial
data.description:
Update: Adds statement from Novo Nordisk.

Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) said Wednesday its weight-loss drug Zepbound (tirzepatide) provided a 47% greater relative weight loss compared to Danish rival Novo Nordisk’s (NVO) Wegovy (semaglutide) in the first head-to-head trial of the two blockbuster weight loss drugs.

In a study sponsored by Lilly, people taking Zepbound lost an average of 20% of their body weight, while those on Wegovy shed 14%. Specifically, participants using Zepbound lost 50.3 lbs (22.8 kg) and participants on Wegovy lost 33.1 lbs (15.0 kg).

At 72 weeks, Zepbound beat Wegovy on both the primary endpoint and all five key secondary endpoints in this 751-person trial (SURMOUNT-5 phase 3b) of adults living with obesity or overweight with at least one weight-related medical problem and without diabetes.

In a key secondary endpoint, 31.6% of people taking Zepbound achieved at least 25% body weight loss compared to 16.1% of those taking Wegovy.

The full results will be published in a peer-reviewed journal and presented at a medical meeting next year, Lilly said.

Tirzepatide is commercialized for adults with obesity or with overweight who also have weight-related medical problems as Zepbound in the U.S. and Mounjaro in some global markets outside the U.S. The drug is also commercialized as Mounjaro for adults with type 2 diabetes in the U.S.

Semaglutide is commercialized as Wegovy for people living with obesity or for adults with overweight who also have weight-related medical problems and Ozempic for people with type 2 diabetes.

Novo Nordisk told Seeking Alpha: "This is a top-line announcement, and we will await the complete data, including the distribution of treatment doses in the two groups, once the trial has been peer-reviewed and/or published. A greater magnitude of weight loss has previously been shown in the clinical trial program with semaglutide 2.4 mg."

The spokesperson further claimed: "In combination with diet and exercise, Wegovy is the only obesity medicine proven to reduce the risk of major cardiovascular events such as death, heart attack, or stroke in adults with known heart disease and with either obesity or overweight. These significant CV events were assessed regardless of change in weight in the landmark SELECT trial."

U.S.-listed shares of Novo Nordisk (NVO) moved marginally premarket. Lilly (LLY) shares rose as much as 2% in trading before U.S. markets opened.

data.title: Crescent Energy subsidiary plans additional debt offering
data.description:
Crescent Energy’s (NYSE:CRGY) indirect subsidiary Crescent Energy Finance plans to offer $300M aggregate principal amount of additional 7.625% Senior Notes due 2032 in a private placement.

The notes are being offered as additional notes under the indenture dated as of March 26, 2024, pursuant to which Crescent Energy Finance has previously issued $700M of 7.625% Senior Notes due 2032.

The additional notes will have substantially identical terms, other than the issue date, the first interest payment date and the initial offering price, as the existing notes.

The notes mature on April 1, 2032 and pay interest at the rate of 7.625% per year, payable on April 1 and October 1 of each year, with interest payments on the additional notes commencing on April 1, 2025.

Net proceeds, along with proceeds from the company’s recent stock offering, will be used to fund the cash portion of the consideration for the previously announced acquisition of Ridgemar (Eagle Ford) LLC as well as to reduce the borrowings outstanding under revolving credit facility.

Shares were down 2.51% premarket on Wednesday.

data.title: Marvell Technology surges as Wall Street praises its 'all in' AI mantra
data.description:
Wall Street firmly believes that the latest results and guidance from Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) are another reason why the artificial intelligence gravy train isn't slowing any time soon.

Shares of the semiconductor company jumped 12% in premarket trading, while competitor Broadcom (AVGO) rose nearly 3%.

"Next year, both custom AI silicon and electro-optics are expected to grow meaningfully, leading to significant upside to our prior estimates," Needham analyst N. Quinn Bolton wrote in a note to clients.

Bolton added that Marvell's AI revenue is now forecast to top the previous $1.5B target by "hundreds of millions of dollars," as the company's two lead customers for custom silicon — believed to be Amazon (AMZN) and Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) — are ramping up, with Amazon likely driving much of the upside.

"AMZN remains on track to ramp its next-gen custom silicon compute solutions in CY25, and we believe MRVL will be the ASIC partner," Bolton wrote, adding that the company's third customer —believed to be Microsoft (MSFT) — is on track for 2026. "This opportunity is expected to be larger than the engagements with its first two customers," Bolton added. He reiterated his Buy rating and upped his price target to $120 from $95 on Marvell.

Citi analyst Atif Malik maintained his Buy rating and said the company has "hit an inflection point" in sales of custom AI chips, notably Amazon's Trainium2. Amazon said on Tuesday that the Trainium2 is now "generally available" and the Trainium3 will come in 2025.

Malik, who upped his price target to $112 from $91, also positively mentioned Marvell's relationship with Amazon, pointing to the expanded five-year partnership announced earlier this week.

KeyBanc Capital Markets analyst John Vinh also upped his price target — to $125 from $95 — and said he was "encouraged" by the Marvell's results and guidance. He also pointed to the fact that Marvell Chief Executive Matt Murphy said he was "all in" on Marvell, amid speculation he could leave for the top job at Intel (INTC).

Financial results, guidance

For the period ending November 2, Marvell said it earned an adjusted $0.43 per share as revenue rose 6.9% year-over-year to come in at $1.52B. Analysts expected $0.41 per share and $1.45B in sales.

Data center revenue accounted for the vast majority of the $1.52B, as sales from that segment rose 98% year-over-year to come in at $1.1B, ahead of the $1.05B estimate.

Looking to the fourth-quarter, Marvell expects revenue to be $1.8B, plus or minus 5%. Adjusted earnings are forecast to be $0.59 per share, plus or minus $0.05. Analysts were expecting $0.52 per share in adjusted earnings and $1.65B in revenue.
